name: ExtensionPlanDefinition_Action_Subject
title: R5: subject additional types
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` 0..1 `canonical, CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` 0..1 `canonical, CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`
*  R4B: `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` 0..1 `canonical, CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`
*  R4: `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` do not cover the following types: canonical.
The target context `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` is a choice-type element and cannot directly hold extensions. The context is moved up to parent element `PlanDefinition.action`.

element.comment:
Element `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` do not cover the following types: canonical.
The target context `PlanDefinition.action.subject[x]` is a choice-type element and cannot directly hold extensions. The context is moved up to parent element `PlanDefinition.action`.
The subject of an action overrides the subject at a parent action or on the root of the PlanDefinition if specified.

In addition, because the subject needs to be resolved during realization, use of subjects in actions (or in the ActivityDefinition referenced by the action) resolves based on the set of subjects supplied in context and by type (i.e. the patient subject would resolve to a resource of type Patient).

element.short: Type of individual the action is focused on
element.definition: A code, group definition, or canonical reference that describes the intended subject of the action and its children, if any. Canonical references are allowed to support the definition of protocols for drug and substance quality specifications, and is allowed to reference a MedicinalProductDefinition, SubstanceDefinition, AdministrableProductDefinition, ManufacturedItemDefinition, or PackagedProductDefinition resource.
element.comment:
The subject of an action overrides the subject at a parent action or on the root of the PlanDefinition if specified.

In addition, because the subject needs to be resolved during realization, use of subjects in actions (or in the ActivityDefinition referenced by the action) resolves based on the set of subjects supplied in context and by type (i.e. the patient subject would resolve to a resource of type Patient).
